pattern: Sort character frequencies and try each frequency as the minimum floor; count deletions needed to bring all others within [floor, floor+k].
hints: A k-special string requires that max_freq - min_freq <= k among all characters still present; characters can be entirely removed (deleting all occurrences).
Sort the distinct character frequencies; for each candidate minimum frequency f, you must delete all occurrences of any character with freq < f, and trim any character with freq > f+k down to f+k.
Try all O(26) unique frequencies as the candidate minimum, compute total deletions for each, and take the minimum.

edge_inputs.description: basic: need to equalize three freqs
k large enough no deletions needed
deleting entire char class can be optimal

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    word = ''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase[:8]) for _ in range(size))
    k = rng.randint(0, 20)
    return [word, k]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def minimumDeletions(self, word, k):
        from collections import Counter
        freq = sorted(Counter(word).values())
        best = float('inf')
        n = len(freq)
        # prefix sums for fast computation
        prefix = [0] * (n + 1)
        for i in range(n):
            prefix[i+1] = prefix[i] + freq[i]
        for i in range(n):
            floor_val = freq[i]
            # delete all chars with freq < floor_val (indices 0..i-1)
            deletions = prefix[i]
            # trim chars with freq > floor_val + k
            ceiling = floor_val + k
            for j in range(i, n):
                if freq[j] > ceiling:
                    deletions += freq[j] - ceiling
            best = min(best, deletions)
        return best
